title: OpenAttribution Manifest
description: Schema for the .well-known/content-telemetry.json manifest defined in section 8 of the OpenAttribution Telemetry specification. A manifest declares a participant's identity, roles, telemetry endpoint, signing keys, and claimed domains.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. v0.1 emitters MUST use '0.1'. (Section 8.2)

roles.description: One or more of content_owner, agent, platform. (Section 8.2)

name.description: Display name of the operating organisation. (Section 8.3)

domain.description: Primary domain. Defaults to the manifest URL's host. (Section 8.3)

id.description: Key identifier, unique within the manifest. (Section 8.4)

publicKey.description: Multibase-encoded public key (multicodec prefix, base58btc - the same format as did:key). (Section 8.4)

keys.description: Public keys for signing telemetry events. Per-event signing is informational in v0.1. (Section 8.4)

endpoint.description: HTTPS URL. For agents and platforms, the outbound submission endpoint. For content owners, the inbound destination for events about the owner's content. (Section 8.5)

conformance_level.description: Conformance level advertised by this participant's own emitter(s). Informational. (Sections 8.5, 5.7)

domains.description: Domains the participant claims authority over. MAY appear only on root manifests; each entry MUST be the manifest's own host or a subdomain of it (literal or wildcard). (Section 8.6)
